title: Partial-id prefix lookup is broken (or CLAUDE.md is wrong)
body:
# Symptom

CLAUDE.md teaches that `jjf` accepts "any unambiguous prefix
(often 4 characters is enough)". In practice, partial-id
prefix lookup does NOT work — every verb that resolves a
handle requires the full 7-char id.

# Repro

In `~/p/jjforge` (the live planner):

```
$ jjf show --json 9566
{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"slug_not_found",
  "message":"no issue with handle \"9566\""}}
$ jjf show --json 9566f5
{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"slug_not_found", ...}}
$ jjf show --json 9566f52
{...full issue payload...}
```

# Verdict — choose one

1. **The feature regressed**: restore prefix lookup. Agents
   and humans routinely abbreviate ids; matching the CLAUDE.md
   contract avoids a UX papercut on every interaction.
2. **The feature never worked**: update CLAUDE.md to remove the
   "often 4 characters is enough" claim, and either (a) document
   that the displayed id IS the full id, no abbreviation, or
   (b) file the feature ask as new work.

# Subordinate issue

When prefix-or-id lookup fails, the error kind is
`slug_not_found`. For a hex-shaped handle this is misleading —
the user typed a (partial) id, not a slug. Splitting into
`id_not_found` / `slug_not_found` / `ambiguous_prefix` (or
renaming to `handle_not_found`) tightens the contract.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100%. Tested in `~/p/jjforge` and in
`scratchpad/qa/r05-race/`.
